expect: history_integrity.py gains a quick mode that reproduces the recent-sample audit path and finishes comfortably within the shell budget on this host while leaving the full report unchanged
actual: Added a first-class --quick preset (25 commits, 20 lessons, last 7 committed sessions), preserved explicit overrides, and parallelized per-lesson git creation lookups in check_lesson_attribution(). On this host the quick audit runtime dropped from 65.362s to 20.973s while producing the same JSON report as the explicit flags --commit-count 25 --sample 20 --min-session 520.
diff: Expected a structural fast path under the shell timeout with unchanged sampled metrics. Actual result exceeded that: the sampled audit is now 3.12x faster (-67.9%) and the quick preset matched the explicit sampled command exactly at the JSON level.

lane_history.design_note: merge-on-close (L-527) deliberately erases per-lane history to prevent bloat
